reasoning: 8x zoom reveals a pixel-art character (cat/creature, green+white palette) with several tells. Most damning: scattered stray pixels in the solid dark background — clusters of off-black and near-white specks that are not part of the sprite and have no compositional purpose (noise tell #3). Outline inspection shows width flicker: the body outline alternates 1px/2px around the head crown and left shoulder, while the right arm edge drops to 0px (missing outline) at two pixel runs — inconsistent outline tell #4. Green leaf/accent patches show low-level chroma bleed into adjacent white pixels along diagonal joins (tell #9). Minor left-right body asymmetry: the lower torso is 1px wider on the right half at row ~18 vs row ~18 left (tell #8). Positive: hard-tone shading (3 discrete values, no gradient bands), palette is disciplined (~8 colors), no obvious melting in the 8x view, semantic read as idle creature sprite is clear. The 4x thumbnail on the left shows acceptable detail merge given size. Background noise and outline inconsistency are the primary failure vectors.
